title: lumen — Architecture
summary: Engineer stable light sources for bands nature does not supply — the next-gen semiconductor-lithography source problem (EUV 13.5nm = first instance).
serve: python3 serve.py  (로컬 서버 + 브라우저 자동 오픈; file:// fetch 차단 우회)

tree.name: lumen
tree.role: Light-source research lab — engineer stable sources for bands nature does not supply (the next-gen semiconductor-litho source problem). Form = research hub + hypothesis lab.
tree.detail: JSON=SSOT (AI/tool parse); humans view architecture.html via `python3 serve.py`. Update in-place (snapshot, not append); history/decisions/dates → CHANGELOG.md + git. One fact per node; deeper detail → child nodes.

children.name: overview
children.role: Engineer stable light sources for bands nature does not supply — the next-generation semiconductor-lithography source problem. EUV (13.5 nm) is the first instance; scope generalizes to any hard-to-generate wavelength lacking a stable natural emitter.

children.name: scope
children.role: The source problem, not one wavelength: EUV is the first instance, not the boundary. Reference fields = light-source, photochemistry, optics.

children.name: core dilemma
children.role: Next-gen lithography wall — three coupled constraints below.

children.name: roadmap gap
children.role: Current EUV has a roadmap through ~3rd generation, but no clear technical alternative beyond it (post-2040s).

children.name: NA vs DoF
children.role: Raising NA (numerical aperture) for resolution shrinks depth-of-focus, forcing extreme-thin resist control → process difficulty and error rate spike.

children.name: shorter-wavelength source
children.role: Going to a shorter wavelength (e.g. 6.5 nm) is theoretically possible, but no clear engineering path to a STABLE source — the central open problem lumen targets.

children.name: components
children.role: Reference fields — the three knowledge pillars of the source problem.

children.name: light source
children.role: Light source (광원) — how the photons are generated. FINAL RANKING below is for the EUV-litho HIGH-FLUX requirement (the binding case); other bands re-rank by their flux requirement (H_040 requirement-driven; H_047 band-regime split).
children.detail: Rank = readiness x robustness as verified (qa.py: 46 verified hypotheses).

children.name: rank 1 — ERL FEL
children.role: RANK 1 — FUNDED near-term answer. A shared CONVENTIONAL energy-recovery linac (ERL) FEL amortized across ~20 scanners (xLight, ~$150M CHIPS, 2028 prototype; KEK 10kW).
children.detail: Clears every EUV-litho wall (wavelength, flux, CAPEX-via-M9 amortization); cost = footprint (large facility, ~40-160m). Demonstrated-class accelerator tech; the industry's revealed bet (H_030/H_042).

children.name: rank 2 — SSMB
children.role: RANK 2 — NOVEL, demonstrated mechanism. Steady-State MicroBunching: a storage ring micro-bunched every turn radiates coherently (P~N^2) and CW (reused beam) → ~1kW @13-14nm (10x HVM floor) at smaller footprint than a full FEL.
children.detail: Mechanism proof-of-principle demonstrated (Deng/Tang/Chao, Nature 2021, MLS); Tsinghua SSMB-EUV funded. Residual: 13.5nm kW undemonstrated (H_043).

children.name: rank 3 — compact SSMB-Compton
children.role: RANK 3 — HIGHEST-UPSIDE / highest-risk (the tabletop dream, resurrected). 7-MeV micro-bunched beam + CO2-laser Compton → 13-14nm from a ~1m metre-scale ring (73x lower energy). Compact + coherent + CW + slice-spread-immune (synthesis of SSMB H_043 + Compton H_033).
children.detail: Gated on ONE milestone: EUV-precision steady-state micro-bunching — physics-ALLOWED (b^2~0.14 at sigma_z~3nm, H_046) but undemonstrated; lab hands off to experiment (H_044-046).

children.name: rank 4 — LPP
children.role: RANK 4 — INCUMBENT baseline (TRL 9, being superseded). LPP (laser-produced tin-droplet plasma) — ASML's current production EUV, ~30yr ecosystem moat.
children.detail: The thing being challenged: hits the 6.5nm optics wall (~8x throughput loss), the flux wall, and the CAPEX-per-wafer wall that drive the whole search. Wall-plug ~0.02% (H_001/H_002/H_020).

children.name: rank 5 — LDP
children.role: RANK 5 — INCUMBENT variant (China indigenous). LDP (laser-induced discharge plasma) — the path ASML abandoned, revisited by China; ~100-150W demonstrated (2025-26), ties LPP on throughput at lower CAPEX. A near-term incumbent-class riser, not a wall-breaker (H_042).

children.name: rank 6 — compact LPA-FEL
children.role: RANK 6 — LONGER-HORIZON, harder (the campaign's original tabletop idea). Compact laser-plasma accelerator + single-pass FEL — wins wavelength tunability but is TERMINAL-THIN on the slice-spread wall (a 0.5% LPA beam is ~5x over the FEL gain criterion; TGU escape insufficient).
children.detail: The LPA driver is the less-mature, less-funded variant; demonstrated LPA-FEL floor ~25nm (H_022/H_031/H_032/H_038).

children.name: rank 7 — recombination laser
children.role: RANK 7 — DARK HORSE. Recombination / inner-shell soft-X-ray laser — intrinsically narrow, undulator-free; harder than its representative anchor (demonstrated 46.9nm gL~8.3 < saturation), undemonstrated at 13.5nm, ~2W (H_017/H_026).

children.name: rank 8 — non-accelerator
children.role: RANK 8 — FAILS the EUV-litho HVM floor. HHG (gas high-harmonic) ~0.30x, DPP (discharge plasma) ~4e-4x the ~100W floor; no non-accelerator family reaches HVM power for litho (confirms the accelerator answer is not first-peak bias). Viable only at lower-flux bands, not EUV-litho (H_042).

children.name: synchrotron (context)
children.role: CONTEXT — conventional synchrotron / storage-ring radiation is the large-facility ancestor of the ranked ERL/SSMB paths (RANK 1-2); high average power but km-scale, not a compact contender on its own.

children.name: photochemistry
children.role: Photochemistry (광화학) — the photoresist that records the image.

children.name: thin resist
children.role: Shrinking depth-of-focus at high NA forces extreme-thin resist; thickness/uniformity control becomes the dominant difficulty and error source.

children.name: optics
children.role: Optics (광학계) — focusing and imaging the source.

children.name: NA / DoF trade-off
children.role: NA ↑ improves physical resolution but shrinks depth-of-focus (DoF); the NA/DoF trade-off drives the resist and process constraints.

children.name: verification (HYPOTHESES)
children.role: HYPOTHESES — hypothesis-verification system (pre-register → falsify → run → verdict), modeled on anima's HYPOTHESES/. Turns pillar open-questions into numerically falsifiable, runnable cards.

children.name: registry
children.role: HYPOTHESES/REGISTRY.jsonl — flat registry, one JSON line per hypothesis (id, slug, tier, title, card, verdict, artifacts).

children.name: cards
children.role: HYPOTHESES/cards/H_*.md — one card per hypothesis: frozen pre-registration + predictions + >=5 measurable falsifiers + honest limits + verbatim run verdict. _TEMPLATE.md is the skeleton.

children.name: harness
children.role: tool/lumen_optics.py — shared deterministic stdlib-only physics harness. Anima-parity: shared machinery lives in repo-root tool/.
children.detail: Functions: mirror_chain_throughput, source_power_multiplier, resolution/DoF, lpp_source_power, hvm_throughput_wph, plasma_omega/wavebreak_field/accelerator_length, undulator/inverse-Compton + Falsifier/evaluate.

children.name: runs
children.role: state/<hX>_<slug>_<date>/run_*.py + result.json — per-hypothesis deterministic run importing tool/; verdict = verbatim stdout (no LLM self-judge).

children.name: abstract lane
children.role: 🜂 ABSTRACT lane — H_A*/H_B* cards (cards/H_[AB]*.md) are unverified conjectures (falsifiable prediction, no run/verdict), tier-separated from verified H_0xx.
children.detail: Sources: brainstorms (state/*-brainstorm.md) + meta-law abstraction (state/euv-meta-laws.md: M1–M5 conserved trades → escapes → predictions). Loop: meta-law → prediction → promote to verified H_0xx (e.g. P4→H_013, H_A4→H_012).

children.name: dataflow
children.role: Form = research hub + hypothesis lab. Tree (this file) is the table of contents; per-pillar research lives flat in state/; open-questions become HYPOTHESES cards.

children.name: input
children.role: Input — sources/devices/papers/specs on the lithography light-source problem.

children.name: curate
children.role: Curate — organize each finding under one of the three reference pillars (light-source · photochemistry · optics).

children.name: output
children.role: Output — structured notes in state/: light-source-paths.md (path comparison table), photochemistry.md, optics.md, euv-source-problem.md (sourced framing).

children.name: verify
children.role: Verify — a pillar open-question becomes a HYPOTHESES card (frozen falsifiers) → runs via tool/ harness in state/ → verdict feeds back into the notes/tree.
